items.title: Switzerland's Crumbling Giants: The alpine towns under threat
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Perth restaurant fined $40k after children accidentally served citronella instead of cranberry juice
items.summary: The former owner of a Perth restaurant is fined after a pair of sisters were served insect repellent instead of the juice they had ordered.

items.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Domestic violence prevention sector rejects NT government's mandatory sentencing bill
items.summary: The Northern Territory government's plan to increase the mandatory minimum sentence for domestic violence murder has been panned by the prevention sector, as the NT attorney-general appears to backtrack on earlier remarks.

items.title: Family doctor had 'sexual motivation' for performing pap smears, tribunal finds
items.summary: A Queensland former doctor has been indefinitely disqualified from reapplying for registration after a tribunal found he excessively performed pap smears, breast checks and vaginal examinations on female patients.

items.title: Jungle Surfing Canopy Tour employees give evidence at inquest into zip-line death
items.summary: The supervisor who was belaying Dean and Shannon Sanderson as they zip-lined between two platforms, recounted the moment he felt the rope go slack and saw the couple drop to the ground.

items.title: Arson attack destroys Mackay tobacconist
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Travis Head rejects claims Australia didn't care about T20 World Cup after early exit
items.summary: Travis Head hits back at the public narrative that has followed Australia's surprise early exit from the T20 World Cup.

items.title: Man previously acquitted of murder confessed on two occasions after becoming Christian, court hears
items.summary: The man is only the second person previously acquitted to be charged with murder under the state's double jeopardy laws, after he allegedly confessed to police twice.

items.title: Sunshine Coast massage therapist Shayne Matthew Harris charged with rape
items.summary: A 58-year-old massage therapist has been charged with sexually assaulting four clients over three years on the Sunshine Coast.

items.title: Jackie O announces departure from breakfast show she hosted with Kyle Sandilands
items.summary: In an ASX announcement, ARN Media saysHenderson "has given notice she cannot continue to work with Mr Sandilands".

items.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: After years in the wilderness, North Korea lays down a marker in Western Sydney
items.summary: Absent from the tournament for 16 years, women's football powerhouse North Korea opens its Asian Cup campaign with victory over Uzbekistan.

items.title: Threats to crucial Strait of Hormuz pack pressure on global oil, gas and freight prices
items.summary: Iran's threats to attack vessels in one of the most important shipping passages will strain global supply of oil and gas, experts say — and Australia will not be immune from the fallout.

items.title: Teenager pleads guilty to threatening to kill prime minister, NSW premier on social media
items.summary: A 19-year-old WA man admits threatening to kill the PM and NSW premier in a direct message to Chris Minns on Instagram.

items.title: Hidden review slams senior Liberals' lack of reflection after devastating election loss
items.summary: Senior decision makers responsible for the Liberal Party's disastrous federal election defeat largely failed to critically assess their own performance in the aftermath, according to a controversial internal review.

items.title: Ross Judd enters not guilty pleas to Mount Waverley double murder
items.summary: Ross Judd appears in the Melbourne Magistrates' Court over the deaths of Athena Georgopoulos and Andrew Gunn in Melbourne's south-east in August.

items.title: Tasmanian government announces Department of State Growth to be replaced by Building Tasmania
items.summary: The Tasmanian government announces it will "transform" an entire department as part of a cost-saving exercise.

items.title: Can you ask a neighbour to remove or trim a large tree for safety or other concerns?
items.summary: Whether you're worried about how old mate's tree next door will hold up in the summer storms, or it's just creating more work for you on the weekend, knowing where you stand can be helpful.

items.title: Man charged with Adelaide Hills assault of father-in-law, who dies from heart attack
items.summary: An Adelaide man who police initially said would be charged with murder has instead appeared in court charged with assault, after a post-mortem examination found the alleged victim died from a heart attack.

items.title: Man dies after being shot by police during welfare check in Brisbane's east
items.summary: Police carrying out a welfare check at a home in Tingalpa shot the 21-year-old man after he allegedly made threats against officers while armed with a knife.

items.title: Queensland government to scrap three-strike drug diversion as doctors question lack of consultation
items.summary: The changes would allow first-time offenders a single chance to be referred to a drug diversion program with subsequent offending attracting fines or criminal charges.

items.title: Matildas star Amy Sayer lauds bravery of Iran players ahead of Asian Cup clash
items.summary: The Matildas are expressing their sympathy while sharing a hotel with Iran's players ahead of this week's crucial Asian Cup clash.

items.title: Police wrongly granted bail to Gold Coast mum Kelly Wilkinson's murderer, inquest hears
items.summary: Queensland Police wrongly granted bail to a man charged with the rape of his estranged wife nine days before he brutally murdered her in 2021, a coronial inquest has heard.

items.title: Tina Arena, Ngaiire reimagine Sia's Unstoppable, open up about life-saving blood donors
items.summary: Music artists Tina Arena and Ngaiire have reimagined Sia's Unstoppable while opening up about how life-saving donated blood brought them back from the brink of serious illness.

items.title: Israel's ambassador to Australia seeks to discredit the United Nations
items.summary: Israel's new ambassador to Australia seeks to discredit the United Nations by claiming the country "can't take them seriously".

items.title: Julian Ingram's court proceedings halted as search for accused triple murderer continues
items.summary: Criminal proceedings against alleged triple murderer Julian Ingram grind to a halt as the five-and-a-half-week-long search for the 37-year-old continues.

items.title: Rory Amon sexual assault trial told ex-MP used fake name to 'manipulate a child'
items.summary: Rory Amon's sexual assault trial nears a conclusion, with the Crown prosecutor saying the complainant's evidence was believable, plausible, consistent and resilient in the face of cross-examination.

items.title: Canberra teenager found not guilty of manslaughter over crash in stolen car that killed 15-year-old
items.summary: A teenage boy accused of driving a stolen vehicle which crashed, killing another teen outside the prime minister's home in Canberra almost two years ago, is found not guilty of manslaughter.

items.title: Australian agriculture hits $100b target four years early but production set to dip next year
items.summary: The value of Australia's farming produce is tipped to reach its "ambitious" $100 billion target this financial year, but experts warn the value could dip again in the face of trying trade and seasonal conditions.

items.title: Troy Maskell jailed after retrial for attack that killed Strathmerton postmaster John Burke
items.summary: Troy Maskell is sentenced to seven years and nine months' prison after a retrial found him guilty a second time of manslaughter over the death of regional Victorian postmaster John Burke.

items.title: Fisheries authorities investigate video of grey nurse shark caught by fishers at Terrigal Haven
items.summary: At least one grey nurse shark is dead and others have been injured by fishers on the NSW Central Coast.

items.title: Chris Scott welcomes Tanner Bruhn's return to Cats after 'egregious injustice' of rape charge
items.summary: Geelong coach Chris Scott confirms Tanner Bruhn will play his first AFL game in 17 months after being sidelined due to rape and intentional sexual touching charges.

items.title: Armed man shot dead by police in Sydney after two women assaulted
items.summary: Police were called to a unit in inner Sydney after an armed man entered the building and assaulted two women.

items.title: Growers branch out as demand grows for unique Australian native flowers
items.summary: Sturt's desert pea may be native to dry, arid conditions but that hasn't stopped growers on the NSW coast from giving it a crack, as demand grows for unique Australian flowers.

items.title: Satellite images show destruction at huge oil refinery, Iranian nuclear facility
items.summary: Newly released satellite imagery shows the aftermath of an Iranian drone attack targeting one of the world's largest oil refineries.

items.title: Calls for review into Queensland fire truck purchases after hundreds of complaints made in 10 months
items.summary: Volunteers say they are being sent to fire grounds in vehicles that fail to meet basic safety requirements.

items.title: Man and woman found dead on rural property at Hernes Oak, near Moe in regional Victoria
items.summary: Police say the pair, who were found dead at a property near Moe on Monday, were known to each other.

items.title: Eels and Storm reach shock out-of-court settlement over Zac Lomax NRL saga
items.summary: Parramatta and Melbourne reach an out-of-court settlement that leaves Zac Lomax unable to join the Storm until 2028.

items.title: Police shoot Newcastle man during apartment block arrest
items.summary: Police have shot a man in Newcastle after an alleged confrontation during an arrest in an apartment block.

items.title: ADF personnel are 'safe' after air base strike near Dubai, Defence Minister Richard Marles says
items.summary: Defence Minister Richard Marles says Australian personnel are safe and accounted for after an air base used by Australian forces was hit by Iranian drones near Dubai.

items.title: Gen alpha is obsessed with all things Asia, and a futurist says they will shape Australia's culture
items.summary: A futurist says Australia will become increasingly influenced by generation alpha and their interest in everything Asian.

items.title: Queensland government makes 11th-hour changes to hate speech laws after criticism
items.summary: The Crisafulli government has introduced last-minute changes to its hate speech laws after widespread criticism.

items.title: Macron says France will allow deployment of nuclear-armed jets to European allies
items.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron says France will expand its nuclear arsenal and will potentially allow European partners to host its aircraft on nuclear deterrence missions.

items.title: Redness on Trump's neck caused by common skin cream, White House says
items.summary: The White House says the large red spot on US President Donald Trump's neck is due to a skin cream he is using, without elaborating on what condition it is treating.

items.title: House panel releases videos of Bill and Hillary Clinton answering questions about Epstein
items.summary: Bill Clinton told the committee that he had ended his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein years before the financier pleaded guilty to soliciting prostitution, and Hillary said she never even recalled meeting him.

items.title: Artists' work at risk as researcher urges privacy checks over AI caricature trend
items.summary: Amateur artist Anne Rowlands lost all her commissions in the past year and says the rise of AI-generated "art" is to blame.

items.title: Rebuilding the scorched nesting hollows of endangered Carnaby's black cockatoos
items.summary: Conservationists are trying to put in place a plan to rehabilitate a key nesting area for endangered black cockatoos that was ravaged by fire.

items.title: National Farmers' Federation vice-president refuses to step down over 'unacceptable' social media posts
items.summary: The National Farmers' Federation board has asked its vice-president to reconsider his position after he made "unacceptable" social media posts.

items.title: Man charged with assault after Adelaide Hills 'disturbance'
items.summary: A man has been charged with assault following the death of a 39-year-old man at Mount Barker in the Adelaide Hills.

items.title: Former friend of Jayson Joseph Michaels shocked by alleged terror attack charges
items.summary: News of 20-year-old Jayson Joseph Michaels being charged with acting in preparation for a "mass casualty" terror act has left his school friends in disbelief.

items.title: NSW Police investigate fourth threatening letter against Lakemba Mosque
items.summary: A three-page "essay of condemnation" addressed to a leader of the Sydney Muslim community sparks renewed fears of escalating threats of violence.

items.title: Soaring oil prices and uncertainty see global stock markets plummet — as it happened
items.summary: Soaring oil prices and economic uncertainty are leading to sharp falls on the ASX and global stock markets. Money markets are also pricing in several more RBA interest rate hikes.

items.title: Nightcliff by-election will be a test for Territory Labor. Can it win it back?
items.summary: Territory Labor lost the previously safe seat of Nightcliff as it was bundled out of government. Eighteen months on, does it deserve to win the seat back?

items.title: Queensland Reds enforcer Lukhan Salakaia-Loto shines to start Super Rugby Pacific season
items.summary: Playing with a chip on his shoulder and a point to prove to national selectors, Lukhan Salakaia-Loto is not wasting any opportunities early in the Super Rugby Pacific season.

items.title: Treasurer asks ACCC to monitor fuel prices amid Middle East conflict — as it happened
items.summary: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has written to the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission asking it to monitor fuel prices amid the unrest in the Middle East. Take a look back on the day's events.

items.title: Good looks drive $235,000 record-breaking sale of South Australian Angus bull
items.summary: Perfect skin, good looks and a sparkling personality may sound like a romantic partner wish list, but this is what buyers are after when selecting a "complete" bull.

items.title: Murder accused refused bail over alleged axe attack at Little Styx River Campground
items.summary: A man accused of murder after a camper's death in an alleged axe attack at a remote campsite in the NSW northern tablelands has had his bail application refused.

items.title: Millions promised for mental health housing support undelivered, years after royal commission
items.summary: The Victorian government is accused of abandoning a funding commitment intended to stop Victorians living with mental illness from falling through the cracks of the housing crisis.

items.title: Booroolong frogs return to north-west NSW waterways thanks to breeding program
items.summary: Hidden in the dark of night, more than 1,000 endangered frogs have been released back into waterways in north-west NSW.

items.title: Trump lists four goals in Iran as Middle East war widens
items.summary: Speaking at his first White House appearance since the joint US-Israeli attack began, the US president says he seized "our last, best chance" to strike Iran's "sick and sinister regime".

items.title: Yorta Yorta leaders claim they were not consulted on $13m project that disturbed ancestral remains
items.summary: Traditional owners in northern Victoria say they were not consulted about a project where human remains, believed to be an ancestral burial, have been unearthed.

items.title: From rule changes to new wildcard round, here's what's new in AFL for 2026
items.summary: The new AFL season is only days away, and there are plenty of new things to get your head around. Get up to speed before Thursday night's opener.
